classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Council in Trullo; Often Called The Quinisext Council

title: Canon LXXIV.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: It is not permitted to hold what are called Agapæ, that is love-feasts, in the Lord’s houses or churches, nor to eat within the house, nor to spread couches.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: If any dare to do so let him cease therefrom or be cut off.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Agapæ are not to be held in the churches, nor shall beds be put up.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Whoso refuse to give up these, let them be cut off.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: This is a renewal of canon xxviij., of Laodicea, on which canon see the notes.
